page: Amazon Web Services t3.micro is a burstable performance instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 1.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 0.5 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the t3.micro sits in the lower performance tiers. In stress-ng CPU tests, it scores 1018.99 for single-core and 1350.29 for multi-core performance, both falling into the bottom 10% category. Memory bandwidth tests show 78.77 GB/sec for cached small blocks, which drops to 6.9 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Passmark CPU tests yield a score of 1633.07, and database operations score at 0.0, indicating significant resource constraints under sustained workloads. However, it manages 1.39 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and 1.76 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3.micro offers an economical entry point for workloads that do not require sustained CPU utilization. Because it uses a burstable CPU allocation model, it is designed to handle temporary spikes in traffic or processing while remaining idle most of the time. This resource density profile makes it suitable for low-traffic web servers, microservices, development environments, and basic testing pipelines rather than resource-intensive database or high-performance computing applications.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.micro is a burstable x86_64 instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 1.0 GB of DDR4 memory, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmark results place this instance in the bottom 10% for compute and memory performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1018.99 and a Passmark CPU score of 1633.07. However, it achieves 1.39 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 1.76 GB/sec in static web serving. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and low memory footprint, the t3.micro is a qualitatively cost-effective option for lightweight, non-sustained workloads such as small development environments, microservices, and low-traffic web applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.micro is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, 2 vCPUs, and 1.0 GB of DDR4 memory, designed for low-intensity and budget-conscious workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.micro is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 1.0 GB of DDR4 RAM for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 instance with two vCPUs and one gigabyte of memory, optimized for lightweight and non-sustained workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
Equipped with 1.0 GB of DDR4 system memory and no local storage
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Delivers 1.39 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and development environments
